key_insight: Theoria verifies AI answers by rewriting them into auditable typed state transitions, so every accepted change must be explicitly justified and hidden premises are exposed.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a practical middle ground between rigid proof assistants and opaque scalar judges: broader coverage with traceable, challengeable verification.
method: Rewrite a candidate solution into a sequence of typed proof-state transitions.
Require each transition to be licensed by an explicit justification such as citation, computation, or problem-given fact.
Enforce completeness of change so any unaccounted difference becomes an unlicensed mutation.
evidence_signal: On 185 HLE-Verified Gold problems, Theoria certified 105 with 91.4% strict precision, and on 95 adversarial poisoned proofs it caught 94.7% versus 83.2% for holistic judging.
watch_item: The approach depends on reliable state rewriting and justification extraction, so implementation quality and domain-specific representation choices may affect coverage and auditability.
paper_title: Theoria: Rewrite-Acceptability Verification over Informal Reasoning States
